content.claim: Hungarian teacher workforce is aging (50% age 50+, only 7.2% under age 30) with critical shortages in rural and disadvantaged areas (unqualified teacher proportion 2-3 times higher in disadvantaged schools), creating compounding institutional strain on any multi-track selective provision model in peripheral regions independent of cohort decline.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Causal link between teacher age structure and selection-track viability not directly evidenced
Rural teacher shortage may reflect multiple factors (housing costs, limited career progression, social infrastructure) beyond aging demographics
No data provided on whether selective tracks face disproportionate or equivalent teacher-shortage impacts versus general secondary provision
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
